page: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 128 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The r7a.32xlarge achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU tests, and software compilation. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with both cached and uncached read-only and write operations ranking in the top tier. This high memory throughput supports rapid data processing, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and text generation benchmarks remain strong, though single-threaded gzip compression falls into the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.32xlarge offers a dense allocation of DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources, making it highly efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, relying on network-attached options. This profile is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, enterprise applications, memory-intensive simulation tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place the r7a.32xlarge in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation. It also demonstrates strong single-core capabilities and outstanding throughput for Redis SET operations. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise database and analytical workloads.

1024 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8 GB per core.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Best suited for in-memory databases, data analytics, and enterprise applications.
